metadata.description: Run the test to confirm the bug

state.title: Run the test to confirm the bug

part.text: The bug is in `addItem`: it assigns `const next = state`, which makes `next` refer to the same object, then mutates the original state via `push`. The fix needs to create a new object with a new array.

metadata.description: Verify the fix passes the test

state.title: Verify the fix passes the test

part.text:
**Fix applied to:** `bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs`

**Bug:** `addItem` assigned `next = state` (same reference), then mutated via `push()`, violating immutability.

**Fix:** Create a new object with spread operator:
```javascript
const next = {
  items: [...state.items, item],
  total: state.total + 1
}
```

**Verification:**
```
node --test bench-targets/react-state/useCartState.test.mjs
# Result: pass 1, fail 0
```
